gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewer Feynman found no correctness or release-safety blockers. The reviewer noted the cached-request fixture could explicitly include resumed_req_ids; I amended the fixture to model the vLLM resumed case and the same reviewer confirmed the note was resolved with no blocker.
verification: python -m pytest -q tests/test_vllm_kv_injection_*.py tests/test_native_probe_factories.py tests/test_adapter_scaffold.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py tests/test_repository_hygiene.py -> 209 passed
changed-file secret-pattern scan for Databricks token and bearer credential patterns -> no matches
GPT-5.5 reviewer found no blockers; residual fixture-precision note was fixed and scoped re-review reported no blocker
what_changed: Hardened DocumentKVNativeProvider scheduler-to-worker metadata so allocated document-KV loads must be present in the current vLLM SchedulerOutput before worker metadata is emitted.
Mapped scheduled new and resumed cached request block IDs explicitly, and reject duplicate or missing scheduled block metadata instead of falling back to stale allocation state.
Added focused tests for new request metadata, resumed cached request metadata, missing scheduler block IDs, and duplicate scheduler block IDs.
why: The provider-backed g6/L4 native path must copy Cachet payload bytes only into the physical KV blocks assigned by the current vLLM scheduler step; failing closed on scheduler-shape drift is safer than producing misleading native-probe evidence.
